structure_en: Write captions in fluent Arabic that express sentiment or wisdom relevant to your content. Include emojis for emotional emphasis. Add 1-2 relevant Arabic hashtags using hashtag format (#word). Structure as: emotional phrase + emoji + newline + hashtag. Target themes of timelessness, relationships, or values that resonate with Saudi audiences.

hashtag_guidance: Add 1-2 relevant Arabic hashtags using hashtag format (#word).
caveats: Requires native or near-native Arabic language proficiency to avoid grammatical errors that damage credibility
Cultural idioms and poetic references must align with Saudi Arabian values and sensibilities
Emoji selection should complement Arabic text without appearing culturally inappropriate or insensitive
Hashtags must use proper Arabic text, not transliteration, to function effectively in algorithmic ranking

extra.description: Content featuring native Arabic captions with emotional or philosophical messaging paired with relevant Arabic hashtags to enhance discoverability and cultural resonance.

structure_en: Use 3-5 emojis per caption (🔥😍✨💛🧡 are frequent choices). Place emojis at line breaks or end of descriptive phrases to punctuate benefits and emotional appeals. Mix Arabic and English if bilingual. Include product name, limited-time messaging, and call-to-action. Structure as 2-4 short lines with emoji punctuation rather than dense paragraphs. Lead with sensory language (طعم غني, ألذ, fluffy) paired with relevant emojis.

structure_en: Open with attention-grabbing emoji (e.g., 🌋 for dramatic products). Insert time-sensitive hook ("Esta semana", "Sep 29–Oct 4"). Use directional emoji (👇) to guide eye flow. List product variants with category-specific emoji (🫐 for berries). Capitalize product names. Include NEW/LIMITED callouts. Stack vertically with emoji prefix for each item. Close with descriptive modifiers ("fresh whipped").

structure_en: Caption structure: [Hook line] + [3-7 numbered or bulleted points] + [CTA or closing line]. Each point is short (5-12 words). Arabic or bilingual (Arabic header, English values). Examples: '3 أسباب تخلي البن السعودي مختلف:' followed by three differentiated points. The list format creates visual rhythm in the caption even before the audience reads each point.

caveats: Points must be genuinely informative — padding a list with obvious or generic points reduces credibility.
Lists work poorly with emotional/heritage content — the structure is too clinical for warm storytelling.
Arabic numbered lists require correct Arabic numerals and right-to-left flow management.
